_source: Promoted from the phase-0 lab corners .scratch/explain-lab-q6-corners.ts (Q6, #185). Each case pins the ratified `proposed` boundary arm; the other six A/B arms stayed in the lab. `truth` is the fully-qualified device path the corner was cross-checked against on the 7.22.1 command tree (the oracle), where the tree can speak. Cases are scored offline with `/` context unless `context` is given. resolution ∈ resolved|ambiguous|unknown; for `resolved`, `path` is the menu and `verb` the verb token; `ambiguous`/`unknown` carry neither.

cases.name: slash menu + space verb — punctuation carries the boundary
all-slash CMD — only the vocabulary recovers the verb
bare menu path — V4: indistinguishable from a no-arg command
cases.rule: bare path, nothing follows — navigation or no-arg command?

cases.name: space-written menu — only the vocabulary recovers this
flag after the verb stays out of the run
`comment=` is an ARG — `=` ends the run (V2)
same word as a VERB — bare, so the vocabulary claims it
directive positional operand — `:global myvar`
cases.rule: R10: a directive's operand is positional

cases.name: numeric operand is not a run token — `:put 1`
cases.rule: R10: a directive's operand is positional

cases.name: bracket ends the run — `:put [/ip/address print]`
cases.rule: R10: a directive's operand is positional

cases.name: no run at all — a bare variable
variable segment truncates the run before the verb
space-written navigation — V4 refuses (the Q4 cascade case)
cases.rule: bare path, nothing follows — navigation or no-arg command?

cases.name: cascade twin — a CMD with the same text shape as `/ip/address`
cases.rule: bare path, nothing follows — navigation or no-arg command?

branches._note: Offline-behavior anchors — they pin ratified `proposed`-arm code paths the oracle corners above do not reach. No device `truth` is asserted: `path` is the rule's DEFINITIONAL output (menu = run before the verb), not a command-tree claim.
branches.name: punctuation first-space token, no vocabulary hit — `[` ends the run
punctuation last segment, no vocabulary hit — all slash-joined, arguments follow
branches.rule: no space token, arguments follow — last segment

branches.name: directive precedence over vocabulary — `:set` is positional, not a CRUD verb
branches.rule: R10: a directive's operand is positional

branches.name: single-token `:log` is NOT the sub-menu case — that needs a second token
branches.rule: R10: a directive's operand is positional

branches.name: KNOWN LIMIT — a bare-word positional arg is absorbed into the run → V4 ambiguous (offline cannot tell `myscript` from a deeper menu; the Q6 residual, cf. the SUT's `enable www-ssl`)
branches.rule: bare path, nothing follows — navigation or no-arg command?

branches.name: `..` navigation is pathresolve.isNav's domain, not this module's — unknown here (a bare `..` is not BARE_WORD-shaped)

contextual.name: relative command inherits the persistent nav context
contextual.rule: R4 context carries into the bare `add`; V4 makes the nav line itself ambiguous

contextual.name: one document, all three verdicts — resolved, ambiguous, unknown
contextual.rule: the fail-closed floor and the ambiguous verdict coexist with resolved commands in a single walk
